label: Fond du Lac Band of Lake Superior Chippewa

comments.text: Yesterday I signed a bill to return 3,400 acres of land to the Fon du Lac Band of Lake Superior Chippewa - a joint effort between the Tribe, the state, and the University of Minnesota.
A large stretch of forest that has been owned by the state of Minnesota for more than 100 years will soon be returned to the Fond du Lac Band of Lake Superior Chippewa.

I was honored to stand alongside the Fond du Lac Band of Lake Superior Chippewa, the University of Minnesota, and legislators as we transferred 3,400 acres of land back to its original caretakers. I’m grateful for the work we’ve done to strengthen Tribal-state relations.

The Fond du Lac Band of Lake Superior Chippewa is the latest tribal nation to sign a cannabis compact with Minnesota, allowing it to license up to 8 off-reservation cannabis retail stores and wholesale to state-licensed businesses. It is the 4th MN tribe to sign a compact (and 2nd this week).

True. Governor Tim Walz signed legislation in late May 2026 returning 3,400 acres of the Cloquet Forestry Center to the Fond du Lac Band of Lake Superior Chippewa. The University of Minnesota will partner with the Tribe to manage the land for research and education.

[June 21st, 1986] The Fond-du-Luth Casino in downtown Duluth, Minnesota, opened through an original joint venture agreement between the City of Duluth and the Fond du Lac Band of Lake Superior Chippewa. This foundational 1986 agreement established revenue sharing between the band and the city.
